original_abstract: „Everyone Knows AI”. A Review of Polish Public Opinion Polls on Artificial Intelligence A study on the perception of artificial intelligence in Poland indicates a growing awareness of this concept. However, many people still have false ideas about this, often confusing AI with automation or robotisation. The survey results suggest that Polish people are confused and do not fully understand what artificial intelligence is. The article presents the results of research based on a systematic review of available internet sources regarding public opinion on artificial intelligence in Poland. A total of 12 different studies, conducted in the six years to 2023, were analysed. The analysis of the research material allowed for drawing five main conclusions regarding: 1) understanding the concept, 2) experiencing the presence of technological artifacts, 3) the scope of acceptance for intelligent machines, 4) the issue of substitutability and 5) confusion. The conclusions from the analysis suggest that Polish people are beginning to understand the role of artificial intelligence, but they still have many questions and concerns about it, and as AI becomes more popular in everyday life, further changes in the way it is perceived can be expected.
humanized_abstract: Literally everybody knows about “artificial intelligence” – how Poles perceive it? The title of this article does not express some irony or at least incomprehension. Our aim is to provide a review of several opinion polls presented in various internet sources and assessing the public opinion in Poland towards artificial intelligence. Thanks to a systematic analysis of on-line materials, which are freely available, we managed to gather 12 independent studies regarding artificial intelligence public opinion in Poland that were conducted in the years 2017–2023. In view of the applied methodology, after analysing the research material, the following conclusions could be formulated: – regarding the comprehension of artificial intelligence as a phenomenon; – regarding the experience of human relationship with technological devices; – concerning the degree and extent of acceptance of intelligent devices by society; – as regards substituting human work by technical means, especially in primary sectors of the national economy; and finally – as concerns the occurrence of confusion, being one of the essential features of public opinion towards this issue. Taking all the mentioned facts into account leads to the conclusion that Poles begin to understand to what extent the emergence of artificial intelligence is crucial to the benefit of the society, yet they still do not fully comprehend how it functions. This study proves that time is necessary for the social acceptance of these advanced technologies. Eventually, AI is going to occupy a permanent place in everyday life, which will necessarily contribute to an altered perception of artificial intelligence.
